title: Scaffold Vite + React Project
description: Use `npm create vite@latest` to bootstrap a React application. Configure the project directory, install dependencies, and run the dev server to confirm the scaffold works.
acceptanceCriteria: Project scaffolded with Vite and React template
Dev server starts without errors
App renders a Hello World in the browser
package.json contains react and react-dom dependencies
hints: Use `npm create vite@latest my-portfolio -- --template react`
Run `npm install` then `npm run dev`
Vite's dev server listens on port 5173 by default

title: Set Up React Router
description: Install `react-router-dom` and configure a root `App.jsx` with a `BrowserRouter`. Define placeholder routes for `/`, `/about`, `/projects`, and `/contact`.
acceptanceCriteria: react-router-dom is installed
BrowserRouter wraps the application root
4 placeholder routes are defined (/, /about, /projects, /contact)
Navigation between routes works without full page reload
hints: Install with `npm install react-router-dom`
Use `<Routes>` and `<Route>` components
Create a `<Navbar>` with `<Link>` elements for client-side navigation

title: Establish Design System (Tokens & Global CSS)
description: Create an `index.css` with CSS custom properties for your color palette (primary, accent, background, text), typography scale (font-size, line-height), and spacing scale. Apply a Google Font.
acceptanceCriteria: CSS custom properties defined under :root (colors, spacing, typography)
Google Font imported and applied globally
Body background and text color use CSS variables
Consistent visual style applied across all pages
hints: Use `--color-primary`, `--color-accent`, `--spacing-md` naming convention
Import Google Fonts via @import in your CSS
Define `font-size` scale using clamp() for fluid typography

title: Build Responsive Navigation Component
description: Create a sticky `Navbar.jsx` component with your name/logo on the left, navigation links on the right, and a hamburger menu for mobile viewports.
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar sticks to top on scroll
Logo/name visible on the left
Navigation links work correctly
Hamburger menu appears on screens < 768px
Hamburger toggles the mobile menu open/closed
Active route is visually highlighted
hints: Use `useState` to track mobile menu open/closed state
Use CSS `@media (max-width: 768px)` for responsive breakpoints
Use `useLocation` from react-router-dom to detect the active route

resources.title: Building a Responsive Navbar with React

title: Build the Hero Section
description: Create a fullscreen hero section with your name, title, a short professional tagline, a Call-To-Action button (View Projects), and your social media links (GitHub, LinkedIn).
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section takes full viewport height
Name and title are displayed prominently
Professional tagline visible
Call-to-action button scrolls to the Projects section
GitHub and LinkedIn icons link to correct profiles
Layout is responsive on all screen sizes
hints: Use `height: 100vh` or `min-height: 100vh` for the hero
Use `scroll-behavior: smooth` on the html element for anchor scroll
Use Lucide React or SVG icons for social links

title: Build the About Section
description: Create a two-column About section with a profile image on one side and a professional bio paragraph and key statistics (years of experience, projects, technologies) on the other.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-column layout (image + bio)
Profile image has a visually styled border or background
Bio text is readable and professional
Statistics chips/cards display correctly
Layout stacks vertically on mobile
hints: Use CSS Grid or Flexbox for the two-column layout
Use `gap` for spacing between columns
Use `border-radius` for circular or rounded profile image

title: Build the Skills Section
description: Create a responsive skills grid displaying technology/skill cards. Each card shows the skill name, an icon (or badge), and a proficiency level indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: Skills displayed in a responsive grid (3-4 columns desktop, 2 mobile)
Each skill has a name and icon/badge
Proficiency indicator (progress bar, dots, or tag) is visible
Grid wraps properly on small screens
Hover effect on skill cards
hints: Use CSS Grid with `repeat(auto-fill, minmax(120px, 1fr))`
Use simple SVG icons or emoji for skill icons
Add `transform: scale(1.05)` on hover with a transition

title: Build the Projects Section
description: Create a Projects grid where each card displays a project title, short description, technology tags, GitHub link, and live demo link.
acceptanceCriteria: Project cards displayed in responsive grid
Each card has title, description, and technology tags
GitHub link opens in a new tab
Live demo link opens in a new tab (if applicable)
Cards have hover effect (lift/shadow)
Technology tags are visually distinct (badges/chips)
hints: Use `target='_blank' rel='noreferrer'` for external links
Define project data as a JS array/object inside the component or a separate data file
Use `box-shadow` with a CSS transition for the card hover lift effect

title: Implement Dark Mode Toggle
description: Add a dark/light mode toggle button in the Navbar. Use CSS custom properties to switch between themes. Persist the preference in localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle button visible in Navbar
Clicking toggles dark/light theme instantly
All sections update their colors to match the selected theme
Theme preference saved to localStorage
Preferred theme restored on page reload
hints: Toggle a `data-theme='dark'` attribute on the `<html>` element
Define dark mode CSS variables under `[data-theme='dark']` selector
Read/write `localStorage.getItem('theme')` on mount

title: Add Scroll Animations
description: Use the Intersection Observer API (or a lightweight library) to fade-in sections as the user scrolls down the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Sections animate in on scroll (fade-in or slide-in)
Animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` media query (no animation for users who prefer reduced motion)
Animations trigger only once per element
No jank or layout shift during animation
hints: Use `new IntersectionObserver()` to detect when elements enter the viewport
Add a CSS class with `opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0)` when the element is visible
Use `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` to disable animations for accessibility

title: Build the Contact Section & Footer
description: Create a contact section with a simple contact form (Name, Email, Message) with client-side validation, and a site footer with social links and copyright text.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact form has Name, Email, Message fields
Client-side validation shows errors for empty or invalid email
Success feedback shown on valid submission
Footer includes social links and copyright text
Form inputs are properly labelled for screen readers
hints: Use `useState` to track form field values and validation errors
Use regex `/^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$/` for email validation
Use `<label htmlFor>` and matching `id` on each input for accessibility

title: Accessibility Audit
description: Run an accessibility audit using browser DevTools Lighthouse. Fix all critical and serious accessibility issues (contrast, labels, alt texts, focus indicators).
acceptanceCriteria: Lighthouse Accessibility score >= 90
All images have meaningful alt attributes
All interactive elements have visible focus indicators
Color contrast ratio >= 4.5:1 for normal text
All form inputs have associated labels
hints: Open Chrome DevTools → Lighthouse tab → Accessibility audit
Use `outline` CSS property for focus indicators instead of removing them
Use WebAIM Contrast Checker for color ratios

title: Optimize for Production
description: Run `npm run build` to generate the production bundle. Verify asset sizes, enable gzip compression in Vite config, and add a `vite.config.js` `base` option for GitHub Pages subdirectory deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Production build completes without errors
Bundle size is reasonable (< 500KB for main JS chunk)
Images are optimized (compressed)
vite.config.js has `base` set to the GitHub repo name
hints: Set `base: '/your-repo-name/'` in vite.config.js for GitHub Pages
Use `npm run build && npm run preview` to test the production build locally
Check the `dist/` folder for generated assets

title: Deploy to GitHub Pages
description: Create a GitHub repository, push your code, and configure GitHub Actions to automatically build and deploy the site to GitHub Pages on every push to main.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub repository created and code pushed
GitHub Actions workflow file created in .github/workflows/
Actions workflow builds and deploys on push to main
Site is live at https://yourusername.github.io/your-repo-name/
All page routes work correctly on the deployed URL
hints: Use the official GitHub Pages Vite deploy workflow from vitejs.dev
Set `GITHUB_TOKEN` permissions to `write` in the workflow file
Enable GitHub Pages in Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions
